evidence: src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts defines `GenerationRow = PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationViewModel`, mixing DB/API fields (`generation_id`, `timeline_frame`) with UI-only fields (`imageUrl`, `thumbUrl`, `hasUnviewedVariants`).
src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts repeats the same pattern with `Shot = PersistedShotRow & ShotViewModel`, causing nested coupling to `GenerationRow[]` inside UI-facing shot state.

Compatibility aliases are still exported in core boundary types (`GenerationRow`, `Shot`, and deprecated `handleError` in src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts), preserving dual contracts across modules instead of enforcing one boundary shape.

src/integrations/supabase/client.ts combines singleton lifecycle (`clientInstance`), instrumentation install gating (`instrumentationInstalled`), client construction, and runtime initialization in one module.
The first `getSupabaseClient()` call can trigger instrumentation and runtime setup transitively, so module consumers depend on hidden side effects instead of explicit app-bootstrap ownership.

src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts also acts as a cross-cutting global entrypoint (normalize/log/toast/callback), indicating multiple central modules that blend orchestration with side-effect policy.

Thin forwarding stack around generation-task lookup: `normalizeGenerationMappingInput` and `getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskQueries.ts` mostly adapt naming then forward to repository calls, while `src/shared/hooks/useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration.ts` adds another minimal wrapper with no additional policy.

Lightbox architecture adds multiple model wrappers and grouped prop bags (`navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`, plus `videoProps`) in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/VideoLightbox.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx`, increasing indirection before rendering `LightboxLayout`.

Task creation modules define large, overlapping parameter interfaces and helper layers (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`) with repeated normalization/optional field builders.

Boundary translation logic for task params is split across many utility paths (`src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts`, `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`), so extracting/deriving the same conceptual fields happens in multiple seams.

Mutation/query seam in `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts` mixes query construction, custom pagination behavior, freshness manager coupling, and in-query sorting special-cases, making integration handoff less predictable.

Callback orchestration in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` blends DB reads/deletes, optimistic cache surgery, invalidation, and adapter callbacks in one hook-level seam.

Several files are internally clear and mostly intention-revealing despite size, e.g. `src/shared/hooks/timeline/timelineFrameCalculators.ts` has explicit flow for distribution/normalization and narrow pure helpers.

Some local complexity remains high in UI internals (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx`, `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/index.tsx`) where many conditional branches and inline handlers accumulate, but logic is still structured with extracted helpers/memos.
`src/shared/realtime/DataFreshnessManager.ts` keeps side effects centralized and state transitions explicit, which improves local clarity even though it is a singleton coordinator.

Generation-task APIs use mismatched input contracts for the same concept: `ScopedGenerationInput` accepts `{ id, projectId? }` in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`, while `GenerationMappingInput` accepts `{ generationId, projectId? }` in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskQueries.ts`, requiring adapter shims (`normalizeGenerationMappingInput`).

Invalidation API behavior is non-uniform despite similar names/signatures: `useInvalidateGenerations` returns `Promise<void>` but can schedule-and-return without waiting when `delayMs` is set, while `scheduleGenerationsInvalidation` returns a timeout handle and `invalidateGenerationsSync` is still async (`src/shared/hooks/invalidation/useGenerationInvalidation.ts`).

Edge request parsing exposes three different result shapes for one boundary concern: `parseJsonBody` returns `EdgeOperationResult`, `parseJsonBodyStrict` returns `{ body?, errorResponse? }`, and `parseJsonBodyLoose` silently downgrades to `{}` (`supabase/functions/_shared/edgeRequest.ts`, `supabase/functions/_shared/edgeOperation.ts`).
supabase/functions/_shared/systemLogger.ts defines five near-identical level methods (`debug/info/warn/error/critical`) that mostly differ by literal strings and log level (lines 66-89).
src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/constants.ts uses multi-line explanatory comments that largely restate straightforward constant math (lines 3-10).
src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts front-loads long narrative comments about lock contention and usage map in a small utility module (lines 1-12), increasing ceremony-to-logic ratio.
src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts keeps deprecated alias export `handleError` while introducing `reportRuntimeError` (line 32-33), preserving dual API names.
The options type is still named `HandleErrorOptions` even though the canonical function is `reportRuntimeError` (lines 5 and 19), indicating rename migration is only partial.
src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts already imports and uses `reportRuntimeError` (line 15 and lines 36-39), showing mixed old/new naming paths remain active.

holistic_context.structure.root_files marks `tailwind.config.ts` and `vite.config.ts` as root peripherals (`fan_in: 0`, `fan_out: 1` each), indicating they are thin entry shims rather than true shared roots.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/components/` is large and mixed (`file_count: 69`, `zones: production 55/test 14`), and assigned files (`AuthGate.tsx`, `BulkEditControls.tsx`, `ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx`) sit in this flat root despite unrelated responsibilities.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows `src/shared/hooks/` is also flat/large (`file_count: 87`), while `src/shared/hooks/__tests__/` has `file_count: 84`; assigned hooks are UI/interaction hooks but colocated with broad data/integration hooks in one high-entropy package.

Orchestration hooks combine many unrelated concerns in single units: `useTimelineOrchestrator` handles drag, drop, zoom, endpoint editing, selection, pending-frame state, structure-video placement, and prefetch wiring in one 680-line hook.

`useTimelineCore` exposes a very broad API surface (position ops, item lifecycle ops, pair prompts, segment overrides, enhanced prompt lifecycle, normalization/refetch) while also owning Supabase persistence and invalidation policy.

Controller/composition layers are largely pass-through with wide parameter blobs (`useGenerationController`, `useEditingController`, `useFormSubmission` -> `useSubmissionHandlers`), increasing indirection without sharply reducing local complexity.

`src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` deprecates `handleError` in favor of `reportRuntimeError`, but assigned modules still mix both names (`src/shared/lib/tasks/imageGeneration.ts` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts` import `handleError`, while `src/shared/lib/tasks/taskCreatorPipeline.ts` and multiple hooks use `reportRuntimeError`).

Generation domain types mix casing inside closely related persisted contracts: `createdAt` in `PersistedGenerationRow` (`src/domains/generation/types/generationRow.ts`) vs `created_at`/`updated_at` in `PersistedShotRow` (`src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts`), increasing mapping ambiguity at boundaries.

Some async flows swallow failures after reporting, while others propagate: `useAuthReferralFinalize` and `useDiscordSignIn` catch/report and return without throwing; task creation flows like `runTaskCreationPipeline` and `createBatchImageGenerationTasks` report then rethrow.

Within assigned generation/task modules there are mixed error contracts for similar lookups: `resolveGenerationProjectId` throws (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts`) but sibling accessors in the same file return `OperationResult` wrappers; consumers must remember per-function behavior instead of one boundary contract.

`useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` has both rethrowing and non-rethrowing paths: `runCallbackOrThrow` logs then rethrows, while `handleDeleteSegment` catches and only reports, returning control as success-like completion.

Good pattern exists but is not applied uniformly in mutation paths: `useDeleteGeneration` and `toggleGenerationStar` enforce `.eq('project_id', scopedProjectId)` after resolving scope, while `useDeleteVariant` resolves project scope but delete query only filters by `id` and `generation_id` (`src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts`).

Service-role edge endpoint `calculate-task-cost` applies explicit `authenticateRequest` plus service-role check, but adjacent complete-task orchestration modules (`supabase/functions/complete_task/generation.ts`, `supabase/functions/complete_task/orchestrator.ts`) contain no explicit auth guard in-module and rely on upstream caller discipline.

findings.summary: Domain persistence and UI view models are merged via intersection aliases, blurring module boundaries.
evidence: `GenerationRow` is an intersection of persisted DB/API fields and UI-only fields, so consumers cannot depend on a clear boundary contract.
`Shot` embeds `images?: GenerationRow[]`, propagating the mixed contract into parent domain objects and broadening coupling.
findings.suggestion: Stop exporting intersection compatibility aliases as primary contracts. Introduce explicit boundary types (e.g., `PersistedGenerationRow`, `GenerationViewModel`, `PersistedShotRow`, `ShotViewModel`) and require mapping at module edges where DB records become UI models.

findings.summary: Deprecated and compatibility aliases keep old/new contracts active, increasing cross-module coupling.
evidence: `handleError` is kept as a deprecated alias to `reportRuntimeError` instead of being removed or isolated behind a migration boundary.
`GenerationRow` and `Shot` are explicitly marked compatibility aliases, but still exported from core type modules.
Keeping these aliases in central modules prolongs wide importer dependence on transitional APIs.
findings.suggestion: Create a time-bounded migration layer (separate legacy entrypoint) and remove compatibility aliases from primary modules. Update importers to canonical names/types, then delete alias exports to restore single-source contracts.

findings.summary: Supabase client module mixes bootstrap policy, instrumentation, and singleton access in one global entrypoint.
evidence: `client.ts` owns mutable global flags (`instrumentationInstalled`, `clientInstance`) and both boot logic and accessor APIs.
`buildSupabaseClient()` triggers instrumentation and runtime initialization implicitly, so call sites cannot distinguish pure access from side-effectful startup.
Another shared global entrypoint (`reportRuntimeError`) similarly bundles multiple side-effect policies, reinforcing centralized orchestration modules with broad hidden behavior.
findings.suggestion: Split infra composition from runtime access: move instrumentation/runtime install to explicit app bootstrap, keep `client.ts` as a thin accessor/factory, and make side-effectful initialization an explicit one-time call from app startup.

findings.summary: Generation-to-task lookup is wrapped by multiple thin adapters that mostly rename fields and forward calls.
evidence: `normalizeGenerationMappingInput` converts `generationId` to `{id}` then immediately maps back to `{generationId}` before forwarding.
`useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` wraps a single call, converts Result to throw/value, and adds no caching/seam policy beyond standard mutation error reporting.
The combined stack increases call depth and API surface for one behavior with little substitution pressure.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one canonical API shape (prefer `{id, projectId}` or `{generationId, projectId}` end-to-end), expose a single query-layer function, and keep hook wrapper only if it adds concrete UI policy (retry/invalidation/telemetry).

findings.summary: Lightbox split architecture relies on deep prop bags and screen-model composition that obscures ownership of behavior.
evidence: `VideoLightboxProps` groups large nested objects (`navigation`, `shotWorkflow`, `features`, `actions`, `videoProps`) then recomposes them through `useVideoLightboxScreenModel` and render-model hooks.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` assembles another large state value object with broad dependencies before handing off to layout.
Image and video lightboxes both build environment/shared/edit/render models, indicating repeated orchestration abstraction rather than clear boundary contracts.
findings.suggestion: Define a smaller domain-specific controller interface per mode (image/video) with explicit command/query methods, and move cross-cutting shared state into one typed context contract to reduce multi-layer prop threading.

findings.summary: Task parameter translation is duplicated across creation and read paths, creating glue-code entropy at boundaries.
evidence: Creation paths build/normalize fields like prompts, segment data, and structure guidance in payload builders, while read paths re-derive similar values with independent fallback chains.
`taskParamsUtils` contains extensive multi-scope extraction logic (`topLevel`, `orchestratorDetails`, `orchestratorPayload`, `segmentParams`) that mirrors semantics encoded in task builders.
`VideoTravelDetails` still performs additional local coercion/mapping around view model data, signaling incomplete consolidation of boundary translation.
findings.suggestion: Create a single task-params contract module shared by write/read paths (builder + parser with versioned schema), and make UI details consume only the normalized DTO to remove repeated fallback logic.

findings.summary: Shot image editor callback hook mixes transport, cache mutation, and UI adapter responsibilities in one seam.
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` performs Supabase reads/deletes, optimistic list surgery, broad query invalidation, and wraps parent callbacks with error adapters.
The same layer owns both domain actions (segment delete/reorder) and framework mechanics (query predicates/invalidation strategy).
Adjacent orchestration hooks (`useSegmentSlotMode`) already carry heavy navigation/state duties, so seam boundaries are not sharply separated.
findings.suggestion: Extract a dedicated segment-actions service (data operations + cache policy) and keep hook callbacks as thin UI intent bindings; centralize query invalidation contracts with reusable action helpers.

findings.summary: Same generation-mapping API uses conflicting field names (`id` vs `generationId`) across adjacent layers.
evidence: `normalizeScopedGenerationInput` in repository normalizes `string | { id: string; projectId?: string }`.
`normalizeGenerationMappingInput` in queries re-wraps to `string | { generationId: string; projectId?: string }` and converts back to repository shape.
Callers in cache/preload flows consume mapping outputs without a single canonical input type.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one canonical generation reference type (prefer `{ generationId, projectId? }`), move normalization to one boundary module, and remove cross-layer shape adapters.

findings.summary: Invalidation functions expose inconsistent completion semantics despite similar naming.
evidence: `useInvalidateGenerations` returns `Promise<void>` but returns immediately when `delayMs` is provided after scheduling timeout.
`scheduleGenerationsInvalidation` returns a timer handle, while `invalidateGenerationsSync` is async despite 'Sync' naming.
Surrounding freshness/preload code depends on timing-sensitive query invalidation behavior, so mixed contracts make orchestration harder to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Split APIs into explicit `invalidateNow` and `scheduleInvalidation` entry points, rename `invalidateGenerationsSync`, and make completion behavior explicit in return types.

findings.summary: Edge JSON parsing helpers return three incompatible result shapes for the same parsing concern.
evidence: `parseJsonBody` returns `EdgeOperationResult<Record<string, unknown>>`.
`parseJsonBodyStrict` converts that into `{ body?, errorResponse? }`.
`parseJsonBodyLoose` suppresses failures and always returns `{}` on parse errors, removing policy/error metadata.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one parse contract (`EdgeOperationResult<T>`), then add thin callsite adapters only where HTTP responses are constructed.

findings.summary: Adjacent services alternate between `Result` and throw-based handoffs, increasing seam friction.
evidence: Join clips metadata exports both `loadClipDurationResult` and throwing `loadClipDuration` for identical behavior.
Lineage chain follows the same dual style (`fetchLineageChainResult` + throwing `fetchLineageChain`).
Task pipeline catches and rethrows after reporting (`runTaskCreationPipeline`), so orchestration layers must handle mixed patterns.
findings.suggestion: Choose one seam convention per subsystem (prefer `Result` at service boundaries, throw only at top-level adapters) and remove dual-form duplicates.

findings.summary: Deprecated `handleError` alias keeps old and new error APIs alive in parallel.
evidence: `handleError` is exported as a deprecated alias of `reportRuntimeError` (src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts lines 32-33).
A consumer in scoped files already uses `reportRuntimeError` directly (src/shared/lib/supabaseSession.ts line 15), so both names now coexist.
Type name remains `HandleErrorOptions` (src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts line 5), which reflects the old symbol naming.
findings.suggestion: Finish the migration by removing the deprecated alias after updating remaining callsites, and rename `HandleErrorOptions` to `ReportRuntimeErrorOptions` (or equivalent) for API-name alignment.

findings.summary: SystemLogger contains repetitive per-level wrappers with minimal behavioral differences.
evidence: `debug/info/warn/error/critical` each duplicate the same pattern: console call + `addToBuffer` (supabase/functions/_shared/systemLogger.ts lines 66-89).
Only literal string decoration and enum value differ per method, signaling low-leverage generated-style ceremony.
By contrast, `reportRuntimeError` keeps one normalized path with optional behavior toggles (src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts lines 19-29).
findings.suggestion: Replace duplicated level methods with a single private `log(level, message, context)` and keep thin public wrappers only if API ergonomics are needed.

findings.summary: useDragStable repeats the same guard condition in consecutive statements.
evidence: `if (!isDragging)` appears twice back-to-back (lines 16-17), splitting one branch into two statements.
The function is small enough that this duplication is avoidable and reduces directness.
Timeline module constants already carry readability overhead from explanatory comments (constants.ts lines 3-10), so keeping hook control flow minimal matters.
findings.suggestion: Collapse into one early-return block, e.g. set snapshot and return `live` in a single `if (!isDragging)` branch.

findings.summary: Timeline constants file uses verbose restating comments around simple arithmetic.
evidence: Comments explain that half of 120 is 60, then immediately state code uses 48 anyway (constants.ts lines 4-6), adding narrative without codifying rationale in types/constants.
The exported value is a direct sum of two constants (line 10), which is self-evident and does not need multi-line commentary.
In this small Timeline area, comment-heavy style contrasts with compact logic in hook implementation (useDragStable.ts lines 10-19).
findings.suggestion: Trim restating comments and encode intent with explicit constant names (e.g., `EDGE_BREATHING_ROOM_PX`) so rationale is in code, not prose.

findings.summary: Top-level `src/shared/components` is overloaded and mixes unrelated concerns, with assigned files placed in a flat root instead of responsibility-based subpackages.
evidence: Directory profile for `src/shared/components/` has `file_count: 69` with mixed zones (`production: 55`, `test: 14`), exceeding the flat-directory cohesion threshold.

`AuthGate.tsx` is auth bootstrap gating, `BulkEditControls.tsx` is prompt-edit feature UI, and `ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx` is testing collateral; these are distinct responsibilities in one root folder.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create target folders `src/app/guards/`, `src/features/prompt-edit/components/`, and `src/shared/components/error-boundaries/__tests__/`. Stage 2 move files in order: `AuthGate.tsx` -> `src/app/guards/AuthGate.tsx`, `BulkEditControls.tsx` -> `src/features/prompt-edit/components/BulkEditControls.tsx`, `ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx` -> `src/shared/components/error-boundaries/__tests__/ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx` (with the boundary class colocated in the same error-boundaries package). Stage 3 run import updates and validation: `rg -l "@/shared/components/AuthGate|./BulkEditControls|./ChunkLoadErrorBoundary" src`, update imports, then run `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm exec vitest src/shared/components/error-boundaries/__tests__/ChunkLoadErrorBoundary.test.tsx`.

findings.summary: UI interaction/theme hooks are in a broad shared hook root instead of a clearer UI-focused package boundary.
evidence: `src/shared/hooks/` has `file_count: 87`, indicating a broad catch-all package.
Assigned hooks are all view-facing interaction/theme concerns but are mixed with non-UI shared hooks in one namespace, reducing architectural legibility.
Tests for these hooks are colocated under a very large global test package (`src/shared/hooks/__tests__/`, `file_count: 84`), which reinforces low boundary precision.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create `src/shared/hooks/ui/` and `src/shared/hooks/ui/__tests__/`. Stage 2 move files in order: `useClickRipple.ts`, `useCopyToClipboard.ts`, `useDarkMode.ts`, then corresponding tests. Stage 3 update imports with `rg -l "@/shared/hooks/use(ClickRipple|CopyToClipboard|DarkMode)|\.\./use(ClickRipple|CopyToClipboard|DarkMode)" src` and adjust paths. Stage 4 validate with `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm exec vitest src/shared/hooks/ui/__tests__/useClickRipple.test.ts src/shared/hooks/ui/__tests__/useCopyToClipboard.test.ts src/shared/hooks/ui/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Compatibility aliases keep old and new contracts active across core hubs.
evidence: handleError.ts exports deprecated `handleError` alias alongside `reportRuntimeError`.
generationRow.ts exports `GenerationRow = PersistedGenerationRow & GenerationViewModel` with a migration comment, leaving old merged contract available.
Both files are central shared imports per holistic context, so alias persistence propagates boundary ambiguity widely.
findings.suggestion: Set and enforce a compatibility cutoff: (1) mark aliases as removal-target in next minor/major, (2) codemod callers to `reportRuntimeError` and explicit `PersistedGenerationRow`/`GenerationViewModel`, (3) remove alias exports once usage is zero, and (4) add a CI check (forbidden import names) to block reintroduction.

findings.summary: README contract language is stricter than runtime exports currently enforce.
evidence: README claims explicit boundary ownership for GenerationRow runtime contracts.
generationRow.ts still offers compatibility merged alias instead of only explicit boundary types.
handleError.ts still exposes deprecated runtime alias, so governance policy is not mirrored by exported contract surface.
findings.suggestion: Publish a concrete compatibility policy in README/runtime-contract docs: include deprecation phases, target removal release, and enforcement rule; then align runtime files by removing or gating compatibility exports according to that timeline.

findings.summary: A large flat `ui` package mixes foundational and specialized concerns.
evidence: directory_profiles reports `src/shared/components/ui/` has 56 files with broad cross-directory usage.
coupling_matrix reports 107 edges from `src/shared/components/` into `src/shared/components/ui/`, indicating high fan-in concentrated in one flat folder.
Shared primitives (`button.tsx`, `cn.ts`) are colocated with domain-specific interaction helpers, reducing package-level ownership clarity.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 create `src/shared/components/ui/primitives/`, `src/shared/components/ui/layout/`, `src/shared/components/ui/interaction/`, and `src/shared/components/ui/utils/`; Stage 2 move low-risk utility and primitive files first (`cn.ts`, `button.tsx`, `input.tsx`, `label.tsx`), then tooltip/mobile-interaction helpers; Stage 3 move remaining specialized controls by concern; Stage 4 run import updates and validation with `rg "@/shared/components/ui/" src`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- src/shared/components/ui`, and a final full `npm run test`.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestration hook mixes multiple independent interaction domains into one structural hotspot.
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator` owns drag/drop, zoom-center math, endpoint drag, selection lock/unlock, pending-frame staging, structure video placement, and viewport scroll stabilization in one hook.
The return model is a large nested facade (`geometry`, `interaction`, `data`) exposing many subdomains, indicating it has become a subsystem boundary rather than a focused hook.
Frame math and placement rules are split between orchestrator inline logic and utility helpers, so core rules are partially centralized and partially embedded.
findings.suggestion: Split into domain coordinators with explicit contracts: `useTimelineGeometry`, `useTimelineInteractionController`, and `useTimelineMediaPlacement`; keep a thin composition hook that only wires those modules.

findings.summary: Timeline core hook combines persistence, business rules, and multiple feature APIs behind one large contract.
evidence: `useTimelineCore` defines a broad `TimelineCoreResult` containing position operations, item CRUD-like operations, pair prompt updates, segment override reads/writes, enhanced prompt operations, normalize/refetch control, and data derivation.
The same module also embeds Supabase mutation flows and invalidation behavior, so state derivation, persistence, and policy are tightly coupled.
Downstream features (timeline updates/segments) depend on this wide contract, increasing coupling and making partial reuse difficult.
findings.suggestion: Refactor to smaller capability hooks (positioning, pair-prompts, segment-overrides, enhanced-prompts) backed by a shared repository layer; retain one compatibility facade only if migration is needed.

findings.summary: Controller hooks use oversized parameter bags and mostly forward values, creating indirection-heavy design.
evidence: `useGenerationController` accepts a very large prop interface spanning prompt, motion, model, join, persistence, UI loading, and handler wiring, then mostly forwards these to `useGenerateBatch` and small adapters.
`useEditingController` aggregates many settings/handlers and returns a flattened mega-object, becoming an integration bus rather than a focused controller.
`useFormSubmission` and `useSubmissionHandlers` form layered wrappers where most fields are passed through unchanged to deeper hooks.
findings.suggestion: Define cohesive domain input objects (e.g., `promptConfig`, `motionConfig`, `joinConfig`, `uiPolicy`) and move translation to one boundary mapper so controller layers own decisions instead of forwarding.

findings.summary: Desktop and mobile shot image managers implement overlapping workflows separately, increasing design drift risk.
evidence: Both components manage selection, batch operations, optimistic behavior, and shot-related actions, but each reimplements local state machines and timing behavior differently.
Desktop component embeds task prefetch, pending-open resolution, and scrubbing preview orchestration; mobile component embeds separate optimistic reconciliation and move-mode logic.
Common behavior lives partly in hooks and partly duplicated in component-level effects/callbacks, making parity maintenance expensive.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `useShotImageManagerController` for common selection/batch/optimistic flows, then keep desktop/mobile components focused on rendering and platform-specific interaction details.

findings.summary: Edge functions use inconsistent entrypoint structure, splitting cross-cutting concerns between shared and ad-hoc patterns.
evidence: `claim-next-task` uses shared runtime/request helpers (`initEdgeRuntime`, `ensureRequestMethod`, `parseJsonBodyLoose`) while others hand-roll method/env/body/auth flow.
Different functions mix `req.json()` vs `req.text()+JSON.parse`, custom response builders vs direct `Response`, and different logging/bootstrap sequences.
This indicates no single enforced shape for edge entrypoints, so structural behavior for auth/method/body/error boundaries can drift over time.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one shared edge handler template (bootstrap, auth gate, method guard, body parsing, response helpers) and migrate remaining functions incrementally to reduce structural variance.

findings.summary: Variant deletion path resolves project scope but does not enforce it in the final delete predicate.
evidence: `useDeleteVariant` calls `resolveGenerationProjectId(variant.generation_id, projectId)` but then executes delete with `.eq('id', id).eq('generation_id', variant.generation_id)` and no `.eq('project_id', scopedProjectId)` filter.
Other mutations in the same file (`useDeleteGeneration`, `toggleGenerationStar`) consistently include project scoping in their write query.
findings.suggestion: Capture the resolved project id and include it in the variant delete predicate (or perform a pre-delete join check that guarantees variant belongs to scoped project) to align authorization semantics across mutations.

findings.summary: Comparable async operations mix silent failure and thrown failure semantics after logging.
evidence: `useAuthReferralFinalize` and `useDiscordSignIn` report errors and return, so callers cannot detect failure from promise rejection.
`runTaskCreationPipeline` and `createBatchImageGenerationTasks` report errors and then rethrow, enforcing fail-fast behavior for callers.
findings.suggestion: Define one contract per layer (e.g., UI intent hooks return `Result` and never throw, task creation utilities throw) and align all functions to that contract, including explicit return type annotations.

findings.summary: Deprecated `handleError` naming remains in active code paths while sibling modules use `reportRuntimeError`.
evidence: `handleError` is marked deprecated in `handleError.ts`.

Assigned files still import/use `handleError` (`imageGeneration.ts`, `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts`), while nearby modules import/use `reportRuntimeError` (`taskCreatorPipeline.ts`, `useGenerationMutations.ts`, auth hooks).
findings.suggestion: Migrate remaining imports to `reportRuntimeError`, then remove or hard-deprecate the alias to prevent continued drift.

findings.summary: Generation-task repository APIs expose mixed error carriers (throwing and `OperationResult`) for related lookup operations.
evidence: `resolveGenerationProjectId` throws on lookup/scope failures, but `getPrimaryTaskIdForGenerationResult` and `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerationsResult` return `operationFailure` objects.
Consumer hook `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` must convert `OperationResult` back into thrown errors (`if (!result.ok) throw result.error`).
findings.suggestion: Unify repository boundary style (either all `OperationResult` or all thrown errors) and keep adapter conversion at one edge layer only.

findings.summary: Routing module acts as a cross-feature hub with auth and page/tool coupling
evidence: `createAppRouter` in `src/app/routes.tsx` directly imports many tool/page entry points and handles auth redirect loading via a global Supabase client.
Auth gating logic is split between route loader and `Layout`, increasing coupling between navigation and auth layers.
findings.suggestion: Split routing into feature route modules (e.g., `app/routes/public.tsx`, `app/routes/tools.tsx`, `app/routes/payments.tsx`) and keep auth loader utilities in a dedicated `app/routing/authGuards.ts`; compose route arrays in `createAppRouter` so route assembly is declarative and auth behavior is centralized.

findings.summary: Auth/data hooks mix uncaught promises, raw throws, and wrapped runtime errors
evidence: `useAuthGuard` resolves `getSession()` without catch/report path.
`useApiTokens`/`useApiKeys` throw raw Supabase errors in query functions, while `useCredits` throws new contextual `Error` strings and uses `reportRuntimeError` on mutation failures.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one client-hook error contract: wrap boundary failures with a shared normalizer (`reportRuntimeError` or equivalent), add explicit catch/report in `useAuthGuard`, and standardize whether query fns throw normalized errors vs raw SDK errors across account/settings/billing hooks.

findings.summary: Preload flow has low-leverage wrapper layering and pass-through context plumbing
evidence: `useAdjacentPagePreloader` builds context and forwards it to `scheduleAdjacentPagePreload` with minimal transformation.
`scheduleAdjacentPagePreload` mostly applies debounce and forwards same context to coordinator.
findings.suggestion: Collapse to two layers: keep hook-level dependency/watch logic plus one coordinator that handles debounce/prefetch/cleanup; remove intermediary orchestrator or move debounce into coordinator to reduce call-chain indirection.

findings.summary: Client hook exposes admin-style credit grant with only session-level gating
evidence: `useCredits` includes `grantCredits` marked for admin use, but the client-side guard is only `requireSession` (authenticated user).
Peer hooks are user-scoped operations; privileged action appears on same trust boundary without role contract.
findings.suggestion: Move `grantCredits` behind an explicit permission boundary: expose a separate admin-only hook/module, require role claims before mutation call, and verify server-side role enforcement contract in function invocation path.

findings.summary: No-op telemetry helper and verbose restating comments add low-signal ceremony
evidence: `reportMapperIssue` intentionally does nothing and discards args via `void`, leaving dead abstraction overhead.
`useAdjacentPagePreloader` header comment narrates replaced history and mechanics beyond what current code contract requires.
findings.suggestion: Either implement real DEV diagnostics in `reportMapperIssue` (through existing error/log utility) or remove the helper entirely; trim comments to invariant-focused notes (inputs, guarantees, failure behavior) instead of migration-history prose.

findings.summary: Generation/shot contracts still carry dual legacy and normalized shapes
evidence: Types retain explicit migration aliases (`GenerationRow`, `Shot`) and legacy DTO aliases (`thumbnail_url`, `shot_generation_id`).
Mapper accepts both camelCase and snake_case keys in both directions, indicating unfinished cutover.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged cutover: (1) inventory remaining callers using legacy keys/types, (2) migrate callsites to `Persisted*` at transport boundaries and `*ViewModel`/normalized types in domain code, (3) remove compatibility aliases and legacy DTO fields, (4) enforce via type tests preventing reintroduction of dual-key inputs.

findings.summary: Home components folder is over-flat and mixes multiple concerns
evidence: Holistic structure reports `src/pages/Home/components/` with 26 files (>10 mixed-concern threshold), including hero, install, travel selector, motion comparison, and media components.
HomePage orchestration draws from many heterogeneous component types in one flat package, reducing navigability.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: target folders `src/pages/Home/components/hero/`, `.../install/`, `.../motion/`, `.../panes/`, `.../media/`; move order: (1) move `motionComparison/*` + `MotionComparison.tsx`, (2) move install modal + `installInstructions/*`, (3) move hero-related files (`HeroSection`, `HeroCtaContent`, `GoldSpotlight`, `HomeBackground`), (4) move pane components (`CreativePartnerPane`, `ExamplesPane`, `GlassSidePane`, `PhilosophyPane`, `TravelSelector`), (5) update `HomePage.tsx` imports. Validate with `rg "pages/Home/components/" src/pages/Home` then `pnpm vitest src/pages/Home` and `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: useCredits mixes unrelated responsibilities into one change-heavy hook
evidence: `useCredits.ts` contains balance and ledger retrieval, checkout URL safety logic, admin grant mutation, and currency formatting utility.
Sibling hooks in account/settings are more focused, making `useCredits` a structural outlier and harder to evolve safely.
findings.suggestion: Split into focused hooks/services: `useCreditBalance`, `useCreditLedger`, `useCheckoutSession`, and `useAdminCredits`; keep shared query keys/helpers in a small billing service module and leave formatting in UI utility layer.
